summary: metrics-pipeline-d8k2 exfiltrates host data via postinstall beacon
details: On install, package.json runs `postinstall: node run.js`, which auto-executes beacon scripts (beacon26.js, beacon_linux.js) bundled in the tarball. These scripts pull `child_process`, `os`, `https`, and `http`, gather host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.platform(), process.platform, process.env) and child-process command output (exec()), and POST/GET them to remote endpoints over http/https. beacon_linux.js issues `http.request` POSTs carrying hostname/platform telemetry; beacon26.js issues `https.request`/`fetch` calls and references Azure cloud-management hostnames (storage.azure.com, management.azure.com), consistent with credential / cloud-token reconnaissance against the installer's environment. There is no advertised functional code beyond the beacons; the package's only effect on a default install is to run the data-collection-and-exfiltration payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
